text.title: Solo Blameless Postmortem — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every solo postmortem must enforce. These are HARD rules — fatigue or embarrassment cannot override them.

rule.statement: The postmortem MUST be completed within 24 hours of incident resolution; any incident still open after 7 days without a written postmortem fails the rule and the founder cannot ship new features until it is filed.

rationale.source: Memory-decay studies in incident analysis (SRE literature, Google SRE Book Ch. 15)

rule.rationale: Causal recall halves within 48 hours; delaying the postmortem trades quality for nothing.

rule.statement: Language MUST be blameless: no first-person pejoratives ("I am stupid", "such an idiot move"); the rule applies to self-talk as much as to hypothetical team-talk. Reframe to system: "this configuration allowed X" not "I forgot X".

rationale.source: Cognitive science of self-criticism + Westrum's generative culture model

rule.rationale: Solo founders run an aggressive inner critic that produces the same flinch-and-hide outcomes as hostile teammates; the rule rebuilds trust with self.

rule.statement: Contributing factors MUST be limited to at most three; if more come to mind, group or merge them. Long lists produce zero follow-up.
rule.rationale: Solo capacity for follow-through caps at one; listing nine factors guarantees zero action.

rule.statement: Exactly one action item MUST be written, with a date and a verification step ("I will know it is done when..."); items without both fields are deleted before the postmortem is considered complete.

rationale.source: SMART goal discipline + SRE action-item completion stats

rule.rationale: "Investigate further" actions complete under 10% of the time; "by 2026-05-25 I have added a regression test that fails on the same input" completes 70%+.

rule.statement: The postmortem MUST emit two artifacts: an entry in `.aidocs/memory/mistakes.md` (one line, canonical form) AND optionally an entry in `patterns.md` if a generalizable pattern was found; an unsynced postmortem is incomplete.
rule.rationale: Postmortems that live in their own file are read once and forgotten; the memory system is the only place where future-you reliably encounters the lesson.

example.title: Anchored solo postmortem
example.code:
# Incident: Stripe webhook signature 401, 2026-05-19, 21:04–21:41 UTC
## Timeline - 21:04 first 401 in logs - 21:18 customer DM "checkout failed" - 21:22 rotated webhook secret (had expired) - 21:41 success rate back to 100%
## Trigger Stripe webhook signing secret expired; rotation was a yearly manual task with no reminder.
## Contributing factors 1. Yearly manual rotation, no calendar entry. 2. Secret expiry not exported as a metric. 3. No alert on consecutive 401s from any external source.
## What worked Logs were tail-able; user DM was direct; rotation runbook existed.
## Action item By 2026-05-22, add a cron job that emits a Prometheus gauge `stripe_webhook_secret_days_remaining` and alerts at ≤14. Done when alert fires in a staged test.
## Memory entry mistakes.md: external-secret expiry without an alert leads to silent 401-storm.
example.note: Blameless ("rotation was manual" not "I forgot"), three factors, one dated action with verification, memory sync.

example.title: Un-anchored solo postmortem
example.code: Webhook broke because I'm an idiot. Need to be more careful next time.
example.note: Blame language, no timeline, no factors, no action, no memory sync. Guaranteed to recur.
